item.title: Lists
item.description:
This is a space reserved for links to all lists tracking the progress of things I watch, read, etc. There may also be lists of things I find interesting. For now, they are only links to the sites because manually adding everything here would be very laborious, although it could serve as a backup. I haven't ruled out this idea, but for now, I'll leave it like this.

My Anime List - Everything related to Animes and Mangas (except light novels)

  • Animes
    • Watching - Animes I'm currently watching;
    • Completed - Animes I've already watched;
    • On Hold - Paused animes;
    • Dropped - Animes I've permanently stopped watching;
    • Plan to Watch - Animes I plan to watch in the future;
  • Mangas
    • Currently Reading - Mangas I'm currently reading;
    • Completed - Mangas I've already read;
    • On Hold - Paused mangas;
    • Dropped - Mangas I've permanently stopped reading;
    • Plan to Read - Mangas I plan to read in the future;

SIMKL - Everything related to TV Series and Movies

  • TV Shows
    • Watching - TV series I'm currently watching;
    • Completed - TV series I've already watched;
    • On Hold - Paused or waiting for new seasons TV series;
    • Dropped - TV series I've permanently stopped watching;
    • Plan to Watch - TV series I plan to watch in the future;
  • Movies
    • Completed - Movies I've already watched;
    • Plan to Watch - Movies I plan to watch in the future;

GoodReads - Everything related to Books and Light Novels

  • Currently Reading - Books I'm currently reading;
  • Read - Books I've already read;
  • Plan to Read - Books I plan to read in the future;

Last.FM - Everything related to Music

  • Scrobbles - Scrobbles from my Spotify playlist (don't use much);
  • Artists - Bands/Artists from my personal playlist;
  • Spotify - My personal playlist on Spotify.

item.title: This Website is Perfect
item.description:
This week, I completed the final steps needed for the website to be "100%." I acquired a '.com' domain and became a supporter of Neocities. Having a domain wasn't something I considered much, but in three days, I gathered information and ended up convinced that it was a very good and relatively inexpensive idea. Supporting the "home" of this website wasn't in the plans, but I liked it so much here that I decided to give it a try. Now, canceling the support is not in the plans.

With Great Domains comes Great Spam

I'm not sure how much this is related to the new domain, but I started receiving more dubious spam in the email I left for contact. It's not really a problem since this email was specifically made for that. However, having to change this email frequently on each page where it is found will be unfeasible going forward: this website is static, each page is made individually, and the more pages there are, the more changes will have to be made. With that in mind, I decided to change the structure a bit by creating pages for specific topics. With this, I can easily change the email on its own page.

But back to the spam, the proposals I received were somewhat absurd, not because they were clearly scams, but for pointing out supposed flaws on this website and offering "better things," a new design, and so on.

I was already preparing to channel my inner James Veitch and respond in the most creative ways that I can only think of when I'm unable to write them down. But I thought twice and gave up. It's not worth it and would be too much work. Maybe when I have more time.

This Website is Already Perfect

I thought a bit about this, whether this was really the website format I would like to have. But everything indicates that yes, at least for now. This website is perfect as it is: simple, responsive, lightweight, and because of that, it has even scored some points in accessibility. It even has RSS! There are plenty of big sites that don't even bother with that...

item.title: About This Project
item.description:
About Me

Hello, I'm JEFBECKER. Some people call me Jeferson, others Becker, so when I needed to choose my first username in 2009, I decided to combine the two. Since then, this has been my "identity" on the internet.

I am an Accountant. I completed college, a technical course, and have been working in the field since 2011, not necessarily in that order. I took a break from studies and work in 2012 due to mandatory military service. It was a lost year, but it resulted in about four friends, some stories, and a aversion to patriotism.

About the Website

This website is my personal space on the internet, free from the constraints of social networks, algorithms, etc. Every line of code here was either made by me (often scratching my head) and/or with the help of various tutorials. I confess that I also peek at the source code of other pages to see how they did "that thing." It's an old idea that I don't exactly remember when it came up, but the main goal has always been to get rid of the Linktree. I used to put lists of what I watch and/or read on various platforms there. It was the solution I found to display these lists as a bulletin board, but I never liked the idea much.

Around 2015, I began to have a growing but slow desire to distance myself from social networks. Since I was never very "social," it wasn't difficult to erase the few traces I left and gradually delete my profiles one by one. In 2021, I discovered Neocities and started learning more about a more open, free, and accessible internet. Everything pointed towards a more self-hosted approach, and after reading a lot about its benefits, I realized I couldn't simply host my own content.

Despite not being able to host a website on my own, I found several free options that would help me with that. After that, I just had to learn how to make a website. Fortunately, this is a well-documented subject out there. This website exists thanks to free tutorials on the internet, such as:

  • Learn How to Make Websites! - A tutorial from Neocities for creating websites;
  • W3 Schools - Various development tutorials.

Additionally, I clarified many doubts with CSS Tricks and Stack Overflow.

This website was made to last, developed with a mobile-first approach to ensure a good experience on any screen, meeting minimum accessibility requirements. The entire structure was planned so that I can simply take everything with me in a folder if I need to change hosts.

Inspirations

Throughout my rediscovery of WEB 1.0 and 2.0 (which I unfortunately was unable to participate in) and what was Geocities, I had several inspirations that finally made me create my own space on the internet:

  • This Page is Design to Last, by Jeff Huang - A manifesto about internet content and its fragile (or even nonexistent) preservation. Like Jeff, I also cleaned up my browser favorites and realized that several links simply didn't work anymore. Fun or informative pages, forum comments, entire websites, things I saved to read later, etc. Everything can be lost very easily, and part of the blame is on the exaggerated complexity that everything needs to have. The text is not perfect, but it was because of it that I concluded that I could create something that lasts. I created this website thinking about its longevity, simplicity, and consequently, accessibility - it's amazing how a website can be more accessible just by being simpler;
  • This is a motherfucking website and This is still a motherfucking website - Two satires on two levels: the first shows how a simple page of a website can be made with the minimum effort possible, and the second, how applying just a little effort makes everything more beautiful and pleasant. I really like the passive-aggressive approach these sites use to illustrate something that people apparently forgot: how a good website should be;
  • Start a Fucking Blog, by Kev Quirk - Following the passive-aggressive line of the two examples above, this website is also an example, but this time about blogs. Places that also got lost over time but still play a very important role: not depending on the "walled gardens" that the big tech companies built with their social networks over the years. Blogs don't have algorithms, ads, and tracking (sometimes), no panel preventing the visitor from accessing something public because they don't have an account, and so on. The list of obstacles is endless, and I don't like any of it;
  • Bring Back Personal Blogging, on The Verge - An interesting article that summarizes all the reasons mentioned earlier.

Personal Preferences

Perhaps my profession doesn't give it away, but I like accounting. Especially personal finance. I'm also interested in technology - it's no wonder I'm enjoying making this website more than putting content on it. In early 2018, I started watching anime for real. Before that, I only watched something sporadic that was popular at the time. I also read some manga and try to read various books. I say "try" because I have a certain difficulty in consuming longer readings. I like comedy series and the infamous "hero movies." It's all on my lists if you want to see.

How to Contact Me and Other Links

Archive;

Contact Page;

Privacy Policy;

Animes, manga, series, movies, and books are documented in my lists;

Donation Page;

Website profile on Neocities;

Mastodon profile.

item.title: Privacy Policy
item.description:
Privacy is important, and this website takes it seriously. There is no tracking, ads, or data collection.

Data Collection

  • This website does not collect visitor data.
  • Neocities has a generic visit count system that does not generate tracking data.
  • When contacting, visitors can choose to use a personal email or a privacy tool to mask the address, such as DuckDuckGo Email Protection, Firefox Relay, or any temporary email service.

It is highly recommended that the visitor does not use a personal email, but if they choose to do so, the address will not be shared with third parties. For cases where the visitor opts to contact with a personal email, the stored data will be: 1. Username; 2. Email address. The use of this data will be considered for private and non-commercial use, in accordance with Art. 4, item I of Law 13.709/18 (General Data Protection Law - LGPD).

Cookies

This website does not store its own tracking cookies or third-party cookies. You can use the Cookie Metrix tool to check if any cookies are being stored.

Terms of Service

No visitor needs to agree to terms of service to access, view, read, or copy any content available on this website.

License Information

All content available on this website is licensed under the Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International (CC BY-NC-SA 4.0), except in cases where this license is not applicable, according to the following terms:

  • Attribution - You must give appropriate credit, provide a link to the license, and indicate if changes were made. You may do so in any reasonable manner, but not in any way that suggests the licensor endorses you or your use;
  • NonCommercial - This content may not be used for commercial purposes;
  • ShareAlike - If you remix, transform, or build upon the material, you must distribute your contributions under the same license as the original;
  • No additional restrictions - You may not apply legal terms or technological measures that legally restrict others from doing anything the license permits.

Neocities Terms of Service

This website is subject to the Terms of Service and Privacy Policy of the Neocities platform.

This page was last updated on 01/12/2024.
